title: rubus conformis
intro: Rubus conformis, also known as the conform thimbleberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family that is native to North America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Rubus conformis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and provide antioxidant protection. Rubus conformis is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and it can also be made into a salve or ointment to be applied topically for wound care.
